ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #14, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jerome Sapiro for and with Robert Menist (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Menist explained that he has been taking care of Joseph for the last 40 years, that Joseph has had no contact with one of the Sisters in 35 years and that Joseph has resided at Shangri-La (Facility in Oregon) since the age of 16. Mr. Menist also addressed the encroachment issues, stated that there is a risk of cutting of benefits in Oregon, explained that Joseph is given $100.00 a month, that if the cash account goes over $2,000.00 at any time Joseph becomes ineligible for benefits and that he was asked to stop sending Joseph money until he hears from the Facility. Mr. Sapiro stated that Article 9 in the Trust permits recovery of advances from principal (encroachments/paying back the Trust for amounts not used by the Beneficiary) and that this has been a permissible approach since the beginning. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley.
